page.title: Narrative Tenses: Past Simple, Past Continuous and Past Perfect
page.subtitle_html: Narrative tenses help tell a story in the past: Past Simple for main events, Past Continuous for background actions, and Past Perfect for actions that happened before another past moment.
page.subtitle_text: Narrative tenses help tell a story in the past: Past Simple for main events, Past Continuous for background actions, and Past Perfect for actions that happened before another past moment.

blocks.body: {"level":"B1","intro":"Use narrative tenses to make the order of past events clear in a story.","rules":[{"label":"PAST SIMPLE","color":"blue","text":"Use Past Simple for the main events in the story.","example":"The phone rang."},{"label":"PAST CONTINUOUS","color":"emerald","text":"Use Past Continuous for background actions or actions in progress.","example":"I was cooking dinner when the phone rang."},{"label":"PAST PERFECT","color":"violet","text":"Use Past Perfect for an action that happened before another past action.","example":"We had already left when the storm started."}]}

{"title":"1. Quick comparison","intro":"Choose the tense by the role of the action in the story.","rows":[{"en":"Past Simple","ua":"main event in the story","note":"She opened the door."},{"en":"Past Continuous","ua":"background action or action in progress","note":"She was walking home when it started to rain."},{"en":"Past Perfect","ua":"earlier action before another past action","note":"He had never flown before he went to Canada."},{"en":"when","ua":"often connects background and event","note":"I was reading when someone knocked."},{"en":"while","ua":"often connects two parallel background actions","note":"The children were playing while their parents were talking."},{"en":"before / after / already","ua":"often show past-before-past order","note":"After they had closed the shop, they went home."}],"warning":"Keep the function clear: main event, background action, or earlier past action."}

{"title":"2. Basic forms","intro":"These are the simple forms needed for B1 narrative tenses.","items":[{"label":"Past Simple","title":"Affirmative / negative / question","subtitle":"She opened the door. / He did not recognize me. / Did they find the bag?"},{"label":"Past Continuous","title":"Background action","subtitle":"I was cooking dinner. / They were watching TV. / Were you sleeping?"},{"label":"Past Perfect","title":"Earlier past action","subtitle":"We had already left. / We had not met before. / Had she left before you arrived?"}]}

{"title":"3. Decision guide","items":["If it is the next main event in the story, use Past Simple.","If it was already in progress at that moment, use Past Continuous.","If it happened before another past event, use Past Perfect.","Use when for a background action plus a shorter event.","Use while for two longer actions happening at the same time.","Use before, after and already when the order of past events matters."]}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"blue","title":"Using Past Simple when the action was background","wrong":"❌ I cooked dinner when the phone rang.","right":"✅ I was cooking dinner when the phone rang."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"emerald","title":"Forgetting the earlier-past function of Past Perfect","wrong":"❌ We already left when the storm started.","right":"✅ We had already left when the storm started."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"violet","title":"Using Past Perfect without a clear earlier past action","wrong":"❌ Yesterday I had watched TV.","right":"✅ Yesterday I watched TV."}]}

{"title":"5. Pages and related topics","items":[{"label":"Tenses","url":"/theory/tenses","current":false},{"label":"Past Simple vs Past Continuous","url":"/theory/tenses/past-simple-vs-past-continuous","current":false},{"label":"Past Perfect","url":"/theory/past-perfect/past-perfect-forms","current":false},{"label":"Narrative Tenses","url":"/theory/tenses/narrative-tenses","current":true}]}
